page: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64-based AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. The instance features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with uncached read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 397.95 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 191.37 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. The Passmark memory score and database operations also rank in the top tier, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 21464.0. Single-core performance is characterized as strong in Geekbench at 1885.0 but average in stress-ng at 1706.7. Cryptographic operations are supported via AES hardware acceleration, though the processor lacks AVX or AMX extensions.

While the server demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations at 67.6 million operations per second, and static web serving at 72.62 GB/sec, it exhibits performance limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference speed for prompt processing on a large 70B model is weak at 5.9 tokens per second, though text generation for the same model remains average at 3.57 tokens per second. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, web serving, and software compilation, offering a balanced resource density without the overhead of SMT.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 397.95 GB/sec and write speeds of 191.37 GB/sec, alongside strong database and Redis performance. However, it exhibits weak performance in large-model LLM inference. This server is suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring balanced compute and memory resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, suited for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, top-tier memory bandwidth, and balanced resources for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on AWS Neoverse-V2
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 397.95 GB/sec read and 191.37 GB/sec write speeds
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for database hosting, Redis operations, and web serving
Reduced efficiency in large-scale LLM inference workloads
